reasoning: GISTEMP temperature anomaly bracket for a single month (October 2025) between 1.10 and 1.14 is a narrow 0.05-degree band. Without precise recent market info (which I failed to efficiently lookup), predicting a specific narrow margin out 1 month in advance generally carries about a 10-15% confidence interval, as typical standard deviations on these monthly variances from year to year/month to month usually spread over a 0.20 to 0.40 C range, giving this roughly a 10-20% base rate if the expected mean centers near here. Current global anomalies have been running between 1.10 and 1.40 recently, so 1.10-1.14 is a plausible bracket but only one of many. I estimate a roughly 12% chance.
